prose:
Frontier orbital energies of ethylene (C2H4)
Property: HOMO, LUMO energies and HOMO–LUMO gap (eV)
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` = B3LYP/def2-TZVP (RI density-fitted)
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/ethylene/ethylene.xyz); single-point only (no re-optimization) → notation DFT//input-XYZ
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model)
Charge / multiplicity: 0 / 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software backend: PySCF 2.x via chemkit `frontier-orbitals` skill
SCF convergence: converged in 6 cycles; SCF stable
Engine warnings: no warnings reported
Live log: calculation output was streamed to a live log file `frontier_<timestamp>.out` in the caller’s cwd as it ran.
Command (from cli_invocation):
/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/ethylene/ethylene.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpz01vllj7/live.json

Results (gas phase, Koopmans orbital energies):
• HOMO energy  = −7.6155818 eV
• LUMO energy  = −0.17986527 eV
→ HOMO–LUMO gap = 7.435716547017943 eV

Method used: chemkit `frontier-orbitals` at B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier `standard`) in PySCF.
